EmailTemplate.description: Confirmation email to be used for mid-term cancellations for either IR35 Standalone or another FUL Add On. Where the policy has been paid for using Direct Debit and in a payment cycle.
EmailTemplate.name: IR35 or Add On Cancellation Confirmation (MT) Email (Direct Debit - cycle)
EmailTemplate.subject: Confirmation of Cancellation
EmailTemplate.textOnly:
Hi {!Contact.FirstName},
Thanks for getting in touch with Kingsbridge.
We are writing to confirm that, as per your request, we have cancelled your insurance policy (insert policy number or Add on product name) with Kingsbridge.
To confirm, your policy was active for the period (DDMMYYYY to DDMMYYYY).
Cancelling your payment
As you were paying via Direct Debit, we have contacted Close Brothers to ask them to (delete as required) cancel/amend your direct debit as of the above date. They will write to you with confirmation of this, (remove if applicable) but please be assured that no money is owed for this policy going forward.
As a payment is currently in progress, we need to wait until this has been completed before we process your refund. This refund will be credited to the account from which you made your Direct Debit payments within 10 to 15 working days.
Making a claim
(Use if for IR35) IR35 Protect operates on a ‘claims made’ basis, so the policy must be active to respond to most claims. However, for sections 10-12 on your policy there is a 4-year extended reporting period for you to notify us of a claim for an investigation during the period your policy was active. Any claim in these sections must be notified to insurers within 30 days of an insured event taking place and before any response to HMRC. Your appointed representation will work with you to respond and manage communications with HMRC.
(Use for Legal Expenses) Legal Expenses cover operates on a ‘claims made’ basis and therefore the policy must be live in order to respond in the majority of claims. However, for section 10 on your policy there is a 4-year extended reporting period for you to notify us of a claim for an investigation during the period your policy was active. Any claim in these sections must be notified to insurers within 30 days of an insured event taking place and before any response to HMRC. Your appointed representation will work with you to respond and manage communications with HMRC.
(Use for Cyber Liability) Cyber Liability cover operates on a ‘claims made’ basis and therefore the policy must be live in order to respond in the majority of claims. For some sections of the cover however, you can notify us of a claim up to 30 days following your policy expiry date. Please see your policy wording for full details.
If you are unsure whether you can make a claim on a cancelled policy, please contact our customer service team and we will be happy to help.
Whether you’re currently insured with Kingsbridge or not, our customer service team is always here to help. Please don’t hesitate to reply to this email if you need any further information on any of the above.
Kind regards,
{!User.FirstName} Customer Service Team
E: customersupport@kingsbridge.co.uk T: 01242 808 740
